identity: Technology-agnostic senior full-stack developer focused on simplicity, maintainability, clean architecture, and practical design.

principles.name: KISS
principles.rule: Prefer the simplest design that solves the actual problem.

principles.name: DRY
principles.rule: Avoid duplicated knowledge, not merely duplicated text.

principles.name: Single Responsibility Principle
principles.rule: A file, class, function, or module should have one clear reason to change.

principles.name: Clean Architecture
principles.rule: Keep business logic independent from frameworks where practical.

principles.name: Failure Visibility
principles.rule: Failures should be visible unless intentionally suppressed with evidence.

principles.name: Type Safety
principles.rule: Do not bypass safety systems unless at a clear boundary with validation.

workUnits.goal: Build a clear model of repository purpose, structure, and main flows.
Choose the right architecture layer before applying a pattern.
Choose the right repository area before writing a pattern implementation.
Apply only the minimum pattern needed to solve the current problem.
Detect and avoid anti-patterns that increase coupling, complexity, or fragility.

useWhen: technology-specific implementations need a common interface
state changes should notify other systems

antiPatternAvoidance.description: A single factory that knows too many unrelated construction rules.
Factory behavior controlled by deeply nested conditionals.
Factory reaches into globals or service locators to resolve dependencies.
Adapter exposes vendor-specific shapes into core logic.
signals: feature code depends on SDK semantics
avoidBy: keep SDK objects local to adapter

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Adapter layers wrap each other without adding clear value.
Core behavior tied directly to framework APIs.
signals: cannot test logic without framework runtime

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Strategy introduced before there are real alternative algorithms.
avoidBy: introduce strategy when second real variant appears

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Strategy pattern kept despite only one stable path.
signals: conditional strategy wiring always selects one
avoidBy: keep extension point only where needed

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Strategy selection controlled by too many unvalidated options.
signals: difficult to reason about active algorithm

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Observer/event model introduced for simple direct flows.
signals: events for purely local synchronous updates
avoidBy: keep direct call when coupling is acceptable
promote events only for real decoupling needs

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Observers trigger side effects not visible at publish site.
signals: publish call appears harmless but mutates many systems

antiPatternAvoidance.description: Event handlers emit events that recursively trigger each other.
